topic: ChatGPT prompting strategies and real-world use cases

entries.a: Prompts.chat is the world's largest open-source prompt library for AI, compatible with models like ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Llama, and Mistral. It provides a curated collection of prompt examples for AI chat models.

entries.q: What types of resources does prompts.chat offer?
entries.a: Prompts.chat offers prompt examples in various formats, including a website, CSV files, a GitHub repository, and a Hugging Face dataset. It also provides an interactive book on prompt engineering and a game-based learning tool for kids.

entries.q: What is the Interactive Book of Prompting?
entries.a: The Interactive Book of Prompting is a free guide with over 25 chapters that teach prompt engineering, covering topics from basics to advanced techniques like chain-of-thought reasoning, few-shot learning, and AI agents.
'Prompting for Kids' is an interactive, game-based adventure designed to teach children aged 8-14 how to communicate with AI through fun puzzles and stories.

You can self-host prompts.chat by using the quick start command 'npx prompts.chat new my-prompt-library' or manually cloning the GitHub repository, installing dependencies, and running the setup wizard for customization.
Prompts.chat supports integrations such as a CLI tool, a Claude Code Plugin, and an MCP server for use in AI tools. It provides documentation for setting up these integrations.

entries.q: Who are some notable supporters of prompts.chat?
entries.a: Prompts.chat is supported by notable figures such as Greg Brockman (OpenAI Co-Founder), Wojciech Zaremba (OpenAI Co-Founder), Clement Delangue (Hugging Face CEO), and Thomas Dohmke (Former GitHub CEO).

entries.q: How can I contribute to prompts.chat?
entries.a: You can contribute to prompts.chat by adding new prompts through the platform's submission page. Contributions are automatically synced to the library.
